title: 500 Keys

jokes.text: It was this or a Porsche!
jokes.setup: Character defending an obviously poor purchase decision
jokes.punchline: Comparing something terrible to a luxury car as if they were equivalent choices
jokes.explanation: Classic Homer logic - treating a terrible decision as if it was a legitimate choice between two good options. Economy is excellent.

jokes.text: D'oh!
jokes.setup: Something has gone wrong (implied)
jokes.punchline: Homer's signature frustrated exclamation
jokes.explanation: Iconic catchphrase with perfect economy and character integration, but low originality as it's been used hundreds of times.

jokes.text: Returned because of a fight over the prenup. / That one there-- bride's dad made a racist toast. / She wanted a cake with blue rosettes, he slept with her sister.
jokes.setup: Explaining why wedding cakes were returned
jokes.punchline: Escalating from normal wedding problems to increasingly dark revelations
jokes.explanation: Excellent escalation structure, each reveal gets darker. Good economy and structure.

jokes.text: She wanted a cake with blue rosettes, he slept with her sister.
jokes.setup: Continuing the pattern of wedding disaster explanations
jokes.punchline: Juxtaposing trivial cake preference with major relationship betrayal
jokes.explanation: Excellent economy and misdirection - the casual delivery makes the infidelity revelation funnier

jokes.text: Cake, will you do me the honor of making my stomach the happiest bag of acid in the world? You will?!
jokes.setup: Homer addressing the cake romantically
jokes.punchline: Describing his stomach as 'bag of acid' in proposal language
jokes.explanation: Brilliantly weird Homer moment - treating cake like a marriage proposal while being grotesquely literal about digestion.

jokes.text: Dad, our lives! / Fine.
jokes.setup: Child desperately pleading for safety
jokes.punchline: Homer's dismissive, grudging agreement to prioritize survival
jokes.explanation: Perfect character moment - Homer's priorities are so warped that even saving lives feels like an inconvenience. Excellent economy.

jokes.text: Morning. Guten Tag.
jokes.setup: Car accident scene with German tourists
jokes.punchline: Polite greeting despite the crash circumstances
jokes.explanation: Mild culture clash humor, competent but unremarkable

jokes.text: Two of your kids are not locked in the car.
jokes.setup: Officer promising good news first
jokes.punchline: Framing one trapped child as good news because two aren't trapped
jokes.explanation: Clever misdirection joke with perfect deadpan delivery. Great structure and economy.

jokes.text: Babies aren't supposed to sleep on their stomach on a cake.
jokes.setup: Concern about baby safety
jokes.punchline: Treating 'on a cake' as just another unsafe sleeping position
jokes.explanation: Brilliant absurdist moment - Marge applying normal parenting advice to completely abnormal situation.

jokes.text: That's the key to my box with the real skull.
jokes.setup: Finding a key with plastic skull decoration
jokes.punchline: Casual revelation that Homer owns actual human remains
jokes.explanation: Excellent dark humor - escalates from silly to genuinely disturbing with perfect casualness.

jokes.text: This is from a gas station men's room. They put the block of wood on it so you don't walk away with it.
jokes.setup: Homer explaining various keys
jokes.punchline: Admitting he would steal a bathroom key if not for the deterrent
jokes.explanation: Good character comedy - Homer's casual admission of would-be petty theft. Solid observational humor.

jokes.text: These are from a key party we went to before we realized what a key party was. We got out of there just in time. / Aw, come on. You'll miss all the sex.
jokes.setup: Homer and Marge innocently went to a key party
jokes.punchline: Barney's disappointment about missing the swinging
jokes.explanation: Classic adult humor for kids' show - good setup/punchline structure with character-appropriate responses.

jokes.text: Homer, stop him! / Stop who, Marge? Be specific.
jokes.setup: Marge urgently asking Homer to stop someone
jokes.punchline: Homer's literal-mindedness in a crisis
jokes.explanation: Perfect Homer character moment - pedantic even in emergencies. Great economy and character integration.

jokes.text: I really didn't want that subscription to The Atlantic.
jokes.setup: Frink retrieving something from mail
jokes.punchline: Intellectual character complaining about highbrow magazine subscription
jokes.explanation: Mild character-based humor. Works but isn't particularly strong or memorable.

jokes.text: With the biting and the chewing on my tuchus. Oh, the laser eyes! Why did I give it to him?
jokes.setup: Frink describing his robot dog invention
jokes.punchline: Escalating list of why the robot is terrible, ending with self-blame
jokes.explanation: Good escalation structure with Frink's characteristic nervous energy and poor decision-making.

jokes.text: I tried to do bad and I did good. Haw-haw.
jokes.setup: Bart intended mischief but accidentally helped people
jokes.punchline: Nelson laughing at Bart's failure to be bad
jokes.explanation: Classic Simpsons irony with good character voices. Nelson's laugh adds impact.

jokes.text: Seriously, you got any beer? No. I heard drowning makes you feel like you're drunk.
jokes.setup: Duffman claiming he doesn't need beer to have fun
jokes.punchline: Immediately asking for beer, then suggesting drowning as alternative
jokes.explanation: Brilliant subversion of Duffman's marketing persona - reveals his desperation and self-destructive tendencies.

jokes.text: The guys at the AA meeting will never believe this.
jokes.setup: Barney excited about brewery access
jokes.punchline: Plans to brag to his addiction recovery group
jokes.explanation: Dark humor about addiction recovery. Perfect character integration for Barney's struggles.

jokes.text: When we see each other again, you'll be an old man and I'll be a baby!
jokes.setup: Homer giving dramatic farewell
jokes.punchline: Completely nonsensical time travel logic
jokes.explanation: Brilliantly absurd Homer logic mixing dramatic movie farewell with impossible aging mechanics.

jokes.text: And these are from sardine cans your father opened. He saved them out of sentimental value.
jokes.setup: Explaining more of Homer's collected keys
jokes.punchline: Homer being sentimental about sardine can keys
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Homer's bizarre attachment to meaningless objects.

jokes.text: I hope I haven't stumbled onto my anniversary present.
jokes.setup: Marge finding a windup toy key
jokes.punchline: Assuming Homer would give such a cheap/childish gift
jokes.explanation: Good setup for Homer's cheapness but fairly predictable punchline

jokes.text: Come back, Pooter Toot! People might be offended by your mild tastelessness!
jokes.setup: Homer trying to recall his offensive train
jokes.punchline: Understating the train's offensiveness as 'mild tastelessness'
jokes.explanation: Good ironic understatement. 'Pooter Toot' name adds to the humor.

jokes.text: It wasn't me, boys. It was the train. / What train, Daddy? / It-It was right there. With the... and the... you know, the...
jokes.setup: Ned trying to explain away offensive music to his religious children
jokes.punchline: Realizing he can't prove the train existed
jokes.explanation: Perfect cringe comedy for Ned's character - religious guilt mixed with parental panic.

jokes.text: Jesus cries blood every time you lie.
jokes.setup: Child responding to father's obvious lie
jokes.punchline: Extremely graphic religious imagery from innocent child
jokes.explanation: Brilliant dark humor contrasting innocent child delivery with horrific religious imagery. Perfect character integration.

jokes.text: Little Miss Play-It-Safe. Have a nice day. Just like yesterday and the day before that and the day before that...
jokes.setup: Bart mocking Lisa for following rules
jokes.punchline: Escalating repetition suggesting infinite boredom
jokes.explanation: Good escalation structure, though the repetition gag is somewhat predictable. Good sibling dynamics.

jokes.text: Nelson, either tell them what they're having for lunch or get off the P.A. / Sloppy Joes! ...and the day before that... / Tater tots! ...and the day before that...
jokes.setup: Nelson continuing his repetitive joke over school PA
jokes.punchline: Skinner trying to make it functional while Nelson persists
jokes.explanation: Excellent callback that makes the previous gag funnier. Good escalation with practical interruption.

jokes.text: Ooh, banned books! / Band books!
jokes.setup: Lisa excited about finding banned books
jokes.punchline: Realizes they're just band books
jokes.explanation: Simple wordplay joke with good economy. Works for Lisa's character but not particularly clever.

jokes.text: Get thee behind me Namuche! That's the Buddhist Satan.
jokes.setup: Lisa resisting temptation with religious language
jokes.punchline: Clarifying she's referencing Buddhist mythology
jokes.explanation: Great character moment showing Lisa's religious knowledge and tendency to over-explain. Good originality.

jokes.text: I've discovered a mystery wrapped in a riddle in the basement of a lousy school!
jokes.setup: Lisa making dramatic discovery announcement
jokes.punchline: Undercutting drama by calling her school lousy
jokes.explanation: Good character humor - Lisa's dramatic tendencies mixed with honest assessment of her school.

jokes.text: And this tooth has no dentin! Everything is fake.
jokes.setup: Lisa discovering increasingly fake classroom elements
jokes.punchline: Applying her scientific knowledge to identify fake tooth
jokes.explanation: Good escalation capper with Lisa's characteristic scientific analysis of absurd situation.

jokes.text: Mother, it is my birthday, not our anniversary. / Well, it's work if they pay you. / They say they're going to.
jokes.setup: Skinner trying to work while mother intrudes
jokes.punchline: Revealing he's not actually paid
jokes.explanation: Perfect Skinner family dysfunction with economic revelation about his job security.

jokes.text: Myra, cancel all my appointments. / You're just pressing a Mento.
jokes.setup: Skinner dramatically canceling appointments
jokes.punchline: Lisa revealing he's using candy as an intercom button
jokes.explanation: Excellent visual gag revealing Skinner's pathetic office setup. Great economy and structure.

jokes.text: Maybe I can find the answer by texting. Yo, Lenny, do you know anything about driving a blimp while intoxica...
jokes.setup: Homer trying to research drunk blimp flying legality
jokes.punchline: Getting distracted mid-text while proving the danger
jokes.explanation: Perfect Homer logic - texting while flying to ask if texting while flying is legal. Great irony.

jokes.text: This train is mocking our intestinal distress!
jokes.setup: Train making rude noises
jokes.punchline: Ned's overly polite description of being mocked
jokes.explanation: Perfect Ned Flanders voice - making crude humor sound academic and polite.

jokes.text: You mean your windup hate crime?
jokes.setup: Someone asking about their toy
jokes.punchline: Moe's blunt assessment of the offensive train
jokes.explanation: Brilliant escalation and economy. Perfect Moe character voice - blunt and dark but accurate.

jokes.text: One Thousand and One Vulgar Marching Band Formations.
jokes.setup: Lisa finding banned books
jokes.punchline: Books about marching band formations being vulgar
jokes.explanation: Clever visual gag playing on expectations. Good originality though somewhat predictable.

jokes.text: Last place anybody would expect to see a moon: the sky.
jokes.setup: Political speech about moon visibility
jokes.punchline: Claiming the sky is unexpected place for moon
jokes.explanation: Brilliantly absurd political double-talk. Perfect satire of meaningless political rhetoric.

jokes.text: And you're my naked fairy-god-boy. / Aw, geez, I hope no one heard you say that. / And so, to this naked fairy-god-boy, I present the key to the city.
jokes.setup: Mayor's inappropriate description of nude Homer
jokes.punchline: Mayor doubling down publicly despite Homer's embarrassment
jokes.explanation: Good escalation structure with perfect cringe comedy. Mayor's obliviousness drives the humor.

jokes.text: I oversee 14 schools, Seymour, and for some reason, I always find myself talking to you about one of the Simpsons kids!
jokes.setup: Chalmers expressing frustration with recurring problems
jokes.punchline: Breaking fourth wall about show's repetitive structure
jokes.explanation: Excellent meta-humor acknowledging the show's own patterns. Good self-awareness without breaking character.

jokes.text: Um, we were just standing here discussing how we have no, uh, secrets. And nothing to hide.
jokes.setup: Skinner trying to act natural when caught
jokes.punchline: Obviously suspicious denial of having secrets
jokes.explanation: Classic suspicious behavior humor. Predictable but well-executed for Skinner's character.

jokes.text: Oh! Oh, the Zoomanity!
jokes.setup: Homer crashing into zoo
jokes.punchline: Pun on 'humanity' while referencing zoo animals
jokes.explanation: Clever wordplay with good economy. Typical Homer response to chaos he's caused.

jokes.text: Ah, yes, the Simpsons: a notch and a bump, two notches and a bump.
jokes.setup: Key maker recognizing family by key patterns
jokes.punchline: Reducing family to technical key specifications
jokes.explanation: Creative character moment showing Springfield's quirky tradespeople. Good technical humor.

jokes.text: How much? / $1.85. If you want that little plastic ring around it, now that's gonna cost you another seven cents. / Keep the change.
jokes.setup: Asking about cost of key duplication
jokes.punchline: Tiny upsell followed by Lisa's generous response
jokes.explanation: Good escalation with Lisa's characteristic politeness. Minor but well-crafted moment.

jokes.text: I bet it's shoddy. / (bookshelf collapses)
jokes.setup: Lisa predicting poor construction
jokes.punchline: Immediate vindication as furniture collapses
jokes.explanation: Classic setup/payoff structure with perfect timing. Predictable but well-executed.

jokes.text: Geez, they're dropping like flies.
jokes.setup: Mannequins falling from crashed blimp
jokes.punchline: Bart's casual observation about what appear to be people falling
jokes.explanation: Bart's callous reaction to apparent carnage fits his character

jokes.text: Brandine, I think we finally gots us something to cut off that umbrilical cord. / Well, then you got one more mouth to feed.
jokes.setup: Finding tool to cut umbilical cord
jokes.punchline: Revealing ongoing neglect of newborn care
jokes.explanation: Brilliant dark humor about rural poverty and neglect. Perfect character integration for the Spucklers.

jokes.text: They've brought nothing but good luck since I got them.
jokes.setup: Homer complaining about being sick of keys
jokes.punchline: Contradicting himself by admitting they've been lucky
jokes.explanation: Homer's typical contradiction between complaint and reality

jokes.text: Things on the ground are out of my jurisdiction.
jokes.setup: Asked to stop runaway train
jokes.punchline: Bizarre interpretation of police authority
jokes.explanation: Perfect Wiggum logic - completely absurd but delivered with authority. Excellent economy.

jokes.text: Things on the ground are out of my jurisdiction
jokes.setup: Asked to stop train on ground
jokes.punchline: Arbitrary jurisdictional limitation
jokes.explanation: Perfect Wiggum incompetence - finding excuse to avoid work

jokes.text: Clancy, is that you? / Oh, uh, hey, Maryann! Uh, how are you at getting pants off the ground?
jokes.setup: Wiggum encountering old crush while pantless
jokes.punchline: Awkwardly asking for help with suggestive phrasing
jokes.explanation: Perfect cringe comedy with unintentional double entendre. Great character embarrassment.

jokes.text: Yeah, I've been held back more times than I can count. Which I guess is why I keep getting held back.
jokes.setup: Nelson admitting academic struggles
jokes.punchline: Circular logic about counting ability and retention
jokes.explanation: Brilliant circular logic joke with perfect Nelson character voice. Self-aware but not too clever.

jokes.text: There's only one bed that's just right for me, the bed with the truth in it. Which is probably the third one.
jokes.setup: Nelson using Goldilocks metaphor about revealing information
jokes.punchline: Applying fairy tale logic to information sharing
jokes.explanation: Creative metaphor mixing but feels forced coming from Nelson's character

jokes.text: It was a cold winter-- two-vest cold.
jokes.setup: Nelson beginning dramatic story
jokes.punchline: Measuring cold by clothing layers needed
jokes.explanation: Great vivid description with excellent economy. Perfect working-class perspective on weather.

jokes.text: Don't drink it!
jokes.setup: Homer lowering bucket to people below
jokes.punchline: Warning them not to drink whatever he's sending down
jokes.explanation: Perfect Homer moment - helping people while inadvertently revealing the bucket contents are disgusting.

jokes.text: Don't drink it!
jokes.setup: Homer lowering bucket to kids below
jokes.punchline: Warning them not to consume whatever he's sending down
jokes.explanation: Homer's typical lack of planning creating dangerous situation

jokes.text: What video game is this like? / Blimp Assassin 3. / For Wii or Xbox? / Xbox. / Original or 360? / Fly the blimp, you spoiled kid!
jokes.setup: Bart asking for video game reference to fly blimp
jokes.punchline: Escalating technical questions while in emergency
jokes.explanation: Excellent escalation showing generational divide. Perfect character voices and timing during crisis.

jokes.text: Boy, stomp on my head! / Yes! And I've got gum on my shoe!
jokes.setup: Homer asking for help in emergency
jokes.punchline: Bart's enthusiasm about getting to stomp on father, plus bonus complaint
jokes.explanation: Perfect father-son dynamic with Bart's joy at violence and typical complaints. Good character integration.

jokes.text: That's not a real arm, it's plastic. / That doesn't mean you're any better than him, young lady. He could be a war hero.
jokes.setup: Lisa pointing out mannequins are fake
jokes.punchline: Marge defending mannequin's honor and service
jokes.explanation: Brilliant character moment - Marge's excessive politeness extending to inanimate objects. Great escalation.

jokes.text: It's all pooted out.
jokes.setup: Blimp has crashed/deflated
jokes.punchline: Homer's childish description of mechanical failure
jokes.explanation: Good callback to earlier juvenile humor. Perfect Homer vocabulary for technical problem.

jokes.text: And I didn't do anything wrong by fleeing the scene and not reporting it and killing that hobo with the big mouth. I might have imagined that last part.
jokes.setup: Otto relieved about not causing tragedy
jokes.punchline: Escalating admissions ending with possible murder confession
jokes.explanation: Brilliant escalation structure with perfect dark comedy. Excellent character integration and economy.

jokes.text: But these pants were good for another day. / That's not your decision. / I'm the boss of my own laundry. / Well, you don't have to sleep next to you!
jokes.setup: Skinner defending pants choice in flashback
jokes.punchline: Mother's brutal personal attack about sleeping arrangements
jokes.explanation: Perfect Skinner family dysfunction with escalating domestic argument. Excellent character integration.

jokes.text: And they didn't prorate.
jokes.setup: Explaining urgency of mannequin return
jokes.punchline: Administrative concern about rental fees driving dangerous scheme
jokes.explanation: Brilliant bureaucratic humor - reducing elaborate scheme to rental policy concern. Perfect economy.

jokes.text: Otto, maybe it was wrong of us to let you think you killed 24 children. Who can say?
jokes.setup: Revelation that Otto was traumatized for years unnecessarily
jokes.punchline: Skinner's casual dismissal of causing severe psychological trauma
jokes.explanation: Exceptional dark comedy - treating traumatizing someone for years as minor moral ambiguity

jokes.text: How could I? She wasn't even born yet. / People have babies!
jokes.setup: Chalmers defending failure to plan for baby
jokes.punchline: Lisa's exasperated statement of obvious fact
jokes.explanation: Great escalation with Lisa's logical frustration at adult incompetence. Good character voices.

jokes.text: Maybe we'll fall real slow like Inception. / Oop, guess not.
jokes.setup: Bart referencing movie physics during fall
jokes.punchline: Reality immediately contradicting pop culture expectation
jokes.explanation: Good meta-humor with perfect timing. Bart's pop culture knowledge meets reality with excellent economy.

tmdb_overview: After finding the keys to every door in Springfield, Lisa finds a hidden room beneath the elementary school.
